entries.note: Attempt 2 addressed the outstanding blocking issue carried over from the feat-macos-deep-link review (RC-2: FeatureBoard DOM-mutation highlight). The React-state fix (highlightedItems: Set<string>) and the RC-3 URL-cleanup fix (history.replaceState in WorkflowDetailRoute) were already committed in commit 42f7e16 by the feat-attention-banner attempt 3 session; those changes are present in the branch. The remaining gap was a label mismatch: the FeatureBoard status-filter select had aria-label='Filter by status' (same as WorkflowList), while the AC-5 test at line 1063 selects by 'Filter items by status'. Fixed by renaming the aria-label in FeatureBoard.tsx to 'Filter items by status', making it unambiguous without requiring a page.getByRole('main') scope guard. Additionally, three new tests were added to workflow-detail.spec.ts by this session: AC-4 subtitle search ('FeatureBoard search filters by displaySubtitle'), AC-2 subtitle render ('FeatureBoard renders displaySubtitle when present'), and AC-2 phase label ('FeatureBoard renders currentPhase label when non-null'). These tests exercise the data-testid='item-subtitle' and data-testid='item-phase' attributes added in the prior session. All ACs (1–10) and all RCs (1–7) are now covered: RC-2 via React-state highlight, RC-3 via explicit replaceState, RC-6 via WorkflowDetailRoute cleanup, RC-7 via cache-invalidation test.
known_risks: The AC-5 status-filter test no longer uses page.getByRole('main') scoping; it relies on the unique 'Filter items by status' label. If WorkflowList ever adopts that same label, the test would become ambiguous again.

The highlightedItems useLayoutEffect dep array omits onSelectItem (stable callback ref) to avoid an infinite loop risk if the parent passes an unstable lambda. An eslint-disable comment was added; if onSelectItem changes identity, the deep-link scroll fires again on every items re-render.

The data-testid attributes on displaySubtitle and currentPhase span elements are test-only markup; they do not add accessibility value. A future accessibility audit may prefer aria-label or role-based selection instead.
